calendar.matters:
Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-02-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robin Orme for Kendall Watson. Emilie Calhoun for Jewelle Knox-Coverson. Sean Kenney for Reverend Robert Smith.

The Court addressed the funding of the Trusts. Ms. Calhoun stated that the transfer was erroneous. The Court stated that the Examiner had raised the issue of notice, that David was noticed, and asked if David's children should be noticed. Ms. Calhoun stated that David's children are Minors so David would receive their notice. Reverend Smith stated that there are no funds in the the charitable trust. Ms. Calhoun stated that weren't many assets and no leftover money. The Court addressed the faxed signatures and stated that it would modify the order to state that they can exercise their fiduciary duties. Ms. Calhoun stated that the Hayes property is set to close. The Court granted the petition (order signed) insofar as it seeks the authority for Reverend Smith and Jewelle Know-Coverson to exercise their fiduciary duties to execute the settlement agreement. Counsel confirmed that once that was done then the litigation would be resolved.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Oct-19-2010 Continued To Nov-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
